page_title: Enable API Gateway for Kubernetes

   Learn how to install custom resource definitions (CRDs) and configure the Helm chart so that you can run Consul API Gateway on your Kubernetes deployment.

# Enable API gateway for Kubernetes


The Consul ships both Consul API Gateway and Standard API Gateway.
Consul version 2.0 introduces the Consul API Gateway controller and the new API Gateway CRDs under the API Group `consul.hashicorp.com`.

<Note>

The Standard API Gateway controller is installed by default and is the recommended way to deploy the API gateway under the API Group `gateway.networking.k8s.io`.

</Note>

The Consul API Gateway controller is installed when you enable the `crds.consulapi.enabled` or `global.openshift.crds.consulapi.enabled` parameter in the `values.yaml` file. The Consul API Gateway controller is recommended only under the following condition:
  1. When the user is using TCPRoute associated to the API Gateway and Gateway CRDs are managed by the platform under version v1 under the API group `gateway.networking.k8s.io` and not supporting TCPRoute CRD installation.


Before you begin the installation process, verify that the environment you are deploying Consul and the API gateway in meets the requirements listed in the [Technical Specifications](/consul/docs/north-south/api-gateway/k8s/tech-specs). Refer to the [Release Notes](/consul/docs/release-notes) for any additional information about the version you are deploying.

1. The Consul Helm chart deploys the Standard/Consul API gateway using the configuration specified in the `values.yaml` file. Refer to [Helm Chart Configuration - `connectInject.apiGateway`](/consul/docs/reference/k8s/helm#apigateway) for information about the Helm chart configuration options. Create a `values.yaml` file for configuring your Consul API gateway deployment and include the following settings:

  <Tabs>

    <Tab heading="Reference configuration to deploy Standard API Gateway only">
    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true
      crds:
        consulapi:
          enabled: false
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: true
    ```

    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="Reference configuration to deploy Consul API Gateway only">
    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
      crds:
        consulapi:
          enabled: true
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: true
    ```

    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="Reference configuration to deploy both Standard and Consul API Gateway">
    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true
      crds:
        consulapi:
          enabled: true
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: true
    ```

    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="OpenShift (OCP <=4.18)">

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true
      openshift:
        enabled: true
        crds:
          consulapi:
            enabled: false
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: true
      cni:
        enabled: true
        logLevel: info
        multus: true
        cniBinDir: "/var/lib/cni/bin"
        cniNetDir: "/etc/kubernetes/cni/net.d"
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="OpenShift (OCP >=4.19)">

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
      openshift:
        enabled: true
        crds:
          consulapi:
            enabled: false
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: true
      cni:
        enabled: true
        logLevel: info
        multus: true
        cniBinDir: "/var/lib/cni/bin"
        cniNetDir: "/etc/kubernetes/cni/net.d"
    ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="GKE Autopilot">

    By default, GKE Autopilot installs [Gateway API resources](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io), so we recommend customizing the `connectInject.apiGateway` stanza to accommodate the pre-installed Gateway API CRDs.

    The following working example enables both Consul Service Mesh and Consul API Gateway on GKE Autopilot. Refer to [`connectInject.agiGateway` in the Helm chart reference](https://developer.hashicorp.com/consul/docs/reference/k8s/helm#v-connectinject-apigateway) for additional information.

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
      crds:
        consulapi:
          enabled: false
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: false
        manageNonStandardCRDs: true
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="Kubernetes Clusters with Gateway CRDs managed by cluster platforms">

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
      crds:
        consulapi:
          enabled: false
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: false
        manageNonStandardCRDs: true
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>
  </Tabs>


## API Gateway Reconciliation

Starting Consul 2.0, Consul only reconciles API gateway resources that meet specific requirements. For Consul to manage an API gateway, the gateway must:

1. Include the required labels in the gateway metadata

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Standard API Gateway">

<CodeBlockConfig>

```yaml
labels:
  component: api-gateway  
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Consul API Gateway">

<CodeBlockConfig>

```yaml
labels:
  component: api-gateway-consul 
```

</CodeBlockConfig>
</Tab>

</Tabs>
2. Specify the correct controller name that matches the gateway type.

Consul validates both criterias before reconciling the gateway object.

## Standard API Gateway

For the standard Kubernetes Gateway API (using API Group `gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1`), the gateway must:
- Set the `gatewayClassName` to reference a GatewayClass with controller name `consul.hashicorp.com/gateway-controller`
- Include the label `component: api-gateway` in the metadata

Example configuration:

<CodeBlockConfig>

```yaml
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: api-gateway # can be modified 
  namespace: consul # can be modified
  labels:
    component: api-gateway # Do not modify this
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

## Consul API Gateway

For the  Consul Gateway resource (using API Group `consul.hashicorp.com/v1beta1`), the gateway must:
- Set the `gatewayClassName` to reference a GatewayClass with controller name `consul.hashicorp.com/gateway-controller-consul`
- Include the label `component: api-gateway-consul` in the metadata

Example configuration:

<CodeBlockConfig>

```yaml
apiVersion: consul.hashicorp.com/v1beta1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: api-gateway-consul # can be modified
  namespace: consul # can be modified
  labels:
    component: api-gateway-consul # Do not modify this
```

</CodeBlockConfig>
